text.title: Value Proposition Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Value Proposition Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Customer-profile jobs MUST cite ≥1 interview citation per job; un-cited jobs rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-cited jobs are founder projection, not customer reality.

rule.statement: Pains MUST describe friction in achieving the job; gains MUST describe outcomes wanted beyond the job — overlap is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Conflating pains and gains hides which side of the funnel each addresses.

rule.statement: Every pain-reliever MUST reference at least one pain id; orphan relievers rejected.
rule.rationale: Relievers without pains are solutions in search of problems.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list every pain/gain with no corresponding reliever/creator under an alignment_gaps array.
rule.rationale: Hidden gaps are the actual root cause of message rejection.

rule.statement: Value-prop statement MUST cite a named customer job; 'faster/easier/better' without job-anchor rejected.
rule.rationale: Adjective stacking is positioning theatre, not positioning.
